features.title: Account Company Normalization Alignment
requirements: Reuse the canonical company normalization behavior already used by Contact matching.
Keep Account-specific weights, caps, and corroboration logic intact unless regression evidence shows a real breakage.
Preserve existing account smoke coverage while adding a focused regression for company alias normalization.
Keep the change compatible with the existing private and shareable branch workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Account matching uses the same canonical company alias logic as Contact matching.
A sparse Account pair that differs only by a company alias and exact phone groups as a duplicate pair.
The existing near-exact name-only Account smoke regression still stays separated.
npm run check, npm run check:windows, and npm run smoke:ui:local pass after the change.

humeDesign.notes: No visible UI redesign is expected for this change.

fixtures: One sparse Account pair using an alias-style company name plus exact phone.
The existing near-exact account smoke fixture.

tests.name: Release pipeline
tests.evidence: Repo closeout now owns release-pipeline verification.

risks: The alias map is intentionally narrow; future broadening should be justified by labeled data.

features.title: Dataset Export With Scores
requirements: Keep the export shape aligned with the imported dataset rather than exporting only group summaries.
Add group and score as appended columns for each original record.
Preserve the source column order and values exactly as loaded.
Use the current computed group assignment and group score from the duplicate matching run.
Keep the export in the existing Export menu so the workflow stays familiar.
acceptanceCriteria: The Export menu includes a Dataset + Scores action.
The exported CSV contains one row per original record.
The exported CSV preserves the original imported columns and appends group and score.
The group value reflects the current duplicate group identifier for each row, or blank when the record is not in a duplicate group.
The score value reflects the current duplicate group score for each row, or blank when the record is not in a duplicate group.
The exported CSV can be re-imported by the existing loader without schema loss.
fixtures: At least one loaded Contacts and one loaded Accounts dataset with duplicate groups and non-duplicate rows so group assignment and blank-group cases can be checked.
Demo data should also produce a valid dataset export with appended group and score columns.
No Salesforce write action is required; this is a local export of the current in-memory dataset state.

tests.name: Release pipeline
tests.evidence: Repo closeout now owns release-pipeline verification.

humeDesign.notes: Reviewed in docs/HUME-DESIGN-REVIEW.md under the existing Export workflow. Keep the new Dataset + Scores action inside the current export menu, preserve the minimalist layout, and avoid adding a new panel or workflow branch.

features.title: Duplicate Reviewer Pull Efficiency Path
requirements: Fail fast before expensive Salesforce pull work when auth freshness or runtime alignment is not usable.
Expose auth freshness and runtime alignment in /api/health.
Resolve the requested org through the canonical shared catalog at the pull boundary.
Reuse an in-flight pull for the same canonical org plus source signature.
Reuse a recently completed pull for the same canonical org plus source signature for a short time window.
Keep the read-only instance URL display and existing apply flow intact.
Surface compact blocked or ready feedback in the existing workflow surface instead of adding a new admin view.
acceptanceCriteria: /api/health reports both auth freshness and runtime alignment.
A stale auth state blocks the pull path before the Salesforce job starts.
A matching in-flight pull request reuses the first request instead of starting a duplicate query.
A matching recent pull request reuses the latest cached result when the short-lived reuse window is still valid.
Canonical org resolution prefers the shared catalog alias and does not regress the existing read-only instance URL display or apply flow.
The compact status summary is visible in the normal workflow surface and clearly explains blocked versus ready state.

humeDesign.notes: Use the existing Source rail and status affordances. Keep the new feedback compact, accessible, and inline with the current org selector surface.

fixtures: A canonical staging org, a legacy staging alias that resolves to the canonical alias, and a synthetic repeated pull signature.
Fake Salesforce CLI and fake org catalog fixtures for contract tests.
Live pull wrappers should still resolve orgs through Salesforce CLI and reuse the same output when the signature matches.

tests.name: Release pipeline
tests.evidence: npm run closeout passed after the manifest and brief statuses were synchronized on 2026-06-15.

features.title: Multi-Group Merge Preview Confirmation
requirements: Replace the editable Contact Merge section with a read-only Review before confirming surface once the queued merge set is ready for confirmation.
Support reviewing multiple merge groups in sequence, using the same left-rail group navigation model as Evaluate plus inline Previous and Next controls.
Do not require the operator to individually confirm each group; provide one overall onscreen Confirm or Cancel decision for the queued merge set.
Keep the current pre-merge freshness check as the gate before any final confirmation UI is shown for the queued set.

Show a concise onscreen preview of the resulting surviving Contact for the current group in view, including the selected master Contact, duplicate Contacts that will be merged into it, and the effective retained field values for the merge-relevant Contact fields shown in the matrix.
acceptanceCriteria: After the merge workflow enters confirmation mode and the queued groups are fresh, the merge workspace shows a Review before confirming surface instead of the editable Contact Merge controls.
The left rail and inline Previous / Next controls move between queued merge-group previews without leaving the merge workflow.
No Salesforce merge request is sent until the user clicks the overall onscreen confirmation button for the queued set.
Clicking the overall Cancel action exits confirmation mode without sending any queued merge request.
Playwright smoke coverage proves the multi-group review surface appears, supports navigation, remains read-only, and blocks merge submission until overall confirmation.

humeDesign.notes: Reviewed in docs/HUME-DESIGN-REVIEW.md under Multi-Group Merge Preview Confirmation. Preserve the Evaluate-style left-rail navigation, use a read-only Review before confirming surface, and keep one overall confirm/cancel decision for the queued merge set.

fixtures: At least three Contact duplicate groups with differing Lead Source, company, phone, and mobile values so multi-group preview navigation is meaningful.

tests.name: Release pipeline
tests.evidence: Repo closeout now owns release-pipeline verification.

features.title: Prod Contacts Autoload
requirements: Add a prod Contacts autoload source alongside the existing staging autoload source.
Expose the prod latest Contacts JSON and CSV endpoints from the server.
Route the reviewer server to the canonical Duplicate Reviewer/prod output tree when the prod launcher starts.
Repair any legacy download-prefixed prod output into the canonical prod tree before the reviewer opens.
Seed the prod latest file into Recent files when the reviewer launches server-backed.
Keep staging Contacts and Accounts autoload behavior unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: autoload=prod-contacts loads the expected prod latest Contacts dataset and labels it as the prod latest file.
The app can fetch /api/prod-contacts/latest.json and /api/prod-contacts/latest.csv.
The prod launcher and server both resolve the canonical Salesforce Pulls/Duplicate Reviewer/prod/Output/prod-contacts path.
The prod launcher repairs any legacy download-prefixed prod output before opening the reviewer.
The prod latest file is available in recent files with a clear prod label.
The staging autoload path still works.
fixtures: A prod Contacts JSON export with Contact rows.
A prod Contacts CSV fallback for the same dataset.
risks: The new prod source must stay isolated from the existing staging dataset labels and endpoints.

humeDesign.notes: No visible UI redesign; preserve the existing loading and review layout.

features.title: Prod Accounts Autoload
requirements: Add a prod Accounts autoload source alongside the existing staging autoload sources.
Expose the prod latest Accounts JSON and CSV endpoints from the server.
Route the reviewer server to the canonical Duplicate Reviewer/prod output tree when the prod Accounts launcher starts.
Keep the prod Accounts launcher on the raw report-latest filenames and fresh-runtime handoff.
Seed the prod latest file into Recent files when the reviewer launches server-backed.
acceptanceCriteria: autoload=prod-accounts loads the expected prod latest Accounts dataset and labels it as the prod latest file.
The app can fetch /api/prod-accounts/latest.json and /api/prod-accounts/latest.csv.
The prod launcher and server both resolve the canonical Salesforce Pulls/Duplicate Reviewer/prod/Output/prod-accounts path.
The prod latest file is available in recent files with a clear prod label.
The staging autoload path still works.
fixtures: A prod Accounts JSON export with Account rows.
A prod Accounts CSV fallback for the same dataset.

tests.name: Fast checks
tests.evidence: npm run check passed in the release pipeline on 2026-06-18.

risks: A stale runtime or missing latest-file endpoint could still leave the prod Accounts handoff pointing at the wrong dataset.

humeDesign.notes: No visible UI redesign; preserve the existing loading and review layout.

features.title: Salesforce Org Selector Reflow
requirements: Keep the Salesforce org selector inside the existing Source rail.
Keep the rail as a contained column so long org labels and URLs do not widen into the workspace pane.
Preserve the existing file import workflow, recent files list, and collapse toggles.
Provide a clear scroll path when the left rail contents exceed the viewport.
Keep the org selector compact and truncation-safe on desktop, with readable stacking behavior on narrow screens.
acceptanceCriteria: The Source rail no longer overlaps the workspace column at supported desktop and mobile widths.
The Source, Match Controls, and Match Groups collapse toggles remain visible and clickable after the org selector renders.
The left rail scrolls when its contents exceed the viewport instead of pushing controls offscreen without a scroll path.
Long org labels, aliases, and instance URLs remain usable without widening the rail.
Existing import, match, and merge behavior remains unchanged.
fixtures: At least one loaded dataset with a selected Salesforce org and at least one long org label or URL value to exercise the truncation-safe layout.
No demo-only fallback should alter the org selector layout.
The org selector must continue to drive the same Salesforce org-scoped requests as before.

humeDesign.notes: Reviewed in docs/HUME-DESIGN-REVIEW.md under Salesforce Org Selector Reflow. Keep the selector inline in Source, make the rail scrollable instead of expanding, and keep collapse toggles reachable.

features.title: Salesforce Org Selector Simplification
requirements: Keep the selector inside the existing Source rail.
Render shared org catalog choices as alias-only options.
Remove the separate editable Alias field from the inline selector.
Render the instance URL as a clearly labeled read-only display control.
Preserve the apply flow and mismatch behavior when a catalog org is selected.
Remove the display cap so every available org entry can render.
acceptanceCriteria: The Source rail shows an alias-only shared org catalog dropdown.
The Alias input is no longer present in the selector.
The instance URL is visible but not editable.
Selecting a catalog org and pressing Use org updates the active target org and mismatch warning state.
The dropdown renders every available catalog entry without truncating the list at five items.
The selector remains compact, scrollable, and hit-testable at supported desktop and mobile widths.
fixtures: At least six complete Salesforce org profiles, one matching the loaded dataset source and several others to verify the list is uncapped.
A loaded dataset whose source org metadata can be compared against the selected target org.
No demo-only fallback should alter the selector layout.

humeDesign.notes: Reviewed in docs/HUME-DESIGN-REVIEW.md under Salesforce Org Selector Simplification. Keep the selector inline in Source, use an alias-only dropdown, and render the instance URL read-only.

features.title: Workspace Export and Restore
requirements: Keep the current CSV dataset loader as the source of truth for the source records.
Save the active review state as a single workspace record in browser persistence and export that same record as JSON.
Restore duplicate/not-duplicate decisions, training labels, field-resolution choices, separated records, merge results, and merge master selections from the workspace JSON.
Keep the existing Decisions and Labels CSV exports available for downstream workflows and backward compatibility.
Keep the workspace controls inside the existing Import and Export menus. Do not add a new panel or separate workflow surface.
acceptanceCriteria: The Import menu includes a Workspace action that opens a JSON file picker.
The Export menu includes a Workspace action that downloads a JSON file.
The exported workspace JSON includes the current dataset metadata plus the saved review state.
Importing a matching workspace file restores the saved review state in a fresh browser context after the same dataset is loaded again.
The current CSV-based dataset import path still works unchanged.
The workspace export/import path remains reachable with the existing menu and keyboard behavior on desktop and mobile.
fixtures: At least one Contacts dataset with duplicate groups and saved review state so the round-trip can prove label and decision restoration.
Demo data should still load normally even if no workspace file has been exported yet.
No Salesforce write action is required; this is a local JSON handoff for review state.

humeDesign.notes: Keep the workspace actions inside the existing Import and Export menus. Do not add a new panel or separate workflow surface, and preserve the current topbar layout and scroll model.
